payload.summary: The weekly review pulse highlights concerns around user experience and performance, functional issues, and financial transactions. Many users have reported glitches, errors, and difficulties with the app's interface, resulting in a frustrating experience. There are also concerns about the accuracy of information, customer support, and transaction fees.
quotes: The app is full of glitches and not showing premium properly. Even stop loss and TP is not triggered. Very poor update from the team.
Serious Concern Regarding Incorrect Auto-Linked Bank Account I recently noticed that my INDmoney account is showing a bank account number and IFSC code that are completely unknown to me.

I am trying to connect with customer care from friday 18 april,2026 from app but there is no option to connect live agent and chat gpt has no option to see any attachment, there is an error show in my us stock portfolio
action_items: Conduct a thorough review of the app's interface and functionality to identify and fix glitches and errors
Improve customer support by providing multiple channels of communication, including live chat and email, and ensuring that customer queries are addressed promptly
Enhance transactional transparency by providing clear and accurate information about fees, charges, and account balances to build trust with users
action_ideas: Conduct a thorough review of the app's interface and functionality to identify and fix glitches and errors
Improve customer support by providing multiple channels of communication, including live chat and email, and ensuring that customer queries are addressed promptly
Enhance transactional transparency by providing clear and accurate information about fees, charges, and account balances to build trust with users

explanation_bullets: A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem their units from a mutual fund scheme.
The fee is usually expressed as a percentage of the redemption amount.
Exit loads are typically deducted from the redemption proceeds and paid to the mutual fund house.
The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money too quickly, allowing the fund manager to maintain a stable portfolio.
Exit loads can vary depending on the mutual fund scheme and the time period for which the units are held.
Investors should check the mutual fund scheme's documents to understand the applicable exit load structure.
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker or brokerage firm for services provided.
It is typically a percentage of the transaction value or a flat fee per transaction.
Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of investment, such as stocks, mutual funds, or real estate.
These fees are usually deducted from the investor's account at the time of transaction.
Brokerage fees can be negotiable, and some firms may offer discounted rates for frequent or high-volume traders.
Investors should review and understand the brokerage fee structure before engaging in any investment activity.

Serious Concern Regarding Incorrect Auto-Linked Bank Account I recently noticed that my INDmoney account is showing a bank account number and IFSC code that are completely unknown to me. I have never added, authorized, or linked this account at any point. This appears to have been auto-linked by the system, which raises serious concerns about account security, data accuracy, and fund safety. I hope the support team resolves this matter urgently and transparently.

INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use. Frequent updates shift menus and navigation, forcing users to relearn the app again and again. Instead of improving usability, many changes create confusion and slow down simple tasks. A finance app should feel stable and predictable. Please focus on consistency and meaningful improvements rather than frequent redesigns.

The USD conversion via ICICI Bank is misleading. The exchange rate shown in the INDMoney app is not the rate actually applied—there’s a difference of about ₹0.30 per dollar, with the bank charging a higher rate. When I contacted ICICI Bank, they stated that since this is a third-party transaction, they cannot address the discrepancy. On the other hand, INDMoney’s support team was unable to provide a clear explanation for the difference in exchange rates.
